ip_outcome_note: Confirmed Pidove via PokemonTCG.io API card swsh2-143; National Pokedex no. 519, Normal/Flying-type pigeon Pokemon, regional bird of Generation V Unova region (Pokemon Black & White).

trivia_bullets.text: Pidove (National Pokedex no. 519, Normal/Flying) is the regional bird of Unova, introduced in Generation V (Pokemon Black & White, 2010). Unova is officially modeled on the New York City metropolitan area, confirmed by director Junichi Masuda. Castelia City mirrors Lower Manhattan; Skyarrow Bridge mirrors the Brooklyn Bridge.
sources: WebSearch: Pokemon Black and White Wikipedia

trivia_bullets.text: Pidove's Japanese name Mamepato comes from mame (beans/birdseed) and hato (pigeon), a homophone of haato (heart) — explaining the heart motif embedded in Pidove's chest design. The line culminates in Unfezant (no. 521), likely based on a pheasant or green pheasant — Japan's national bird.
sources: WebSearch: GamesRadar 'Pokemon Black and White Pokedex — Pidove, Tranquill, Unfezant'

trivia_bullets.text: "Where people go, these Pokémon follow. If you're scattering food for them, be careful — several hundred of them can gather at once." The flock-behavior mirrors real urban pigeon behavior in dense cities, grounding the Unova-as-NYC design in observable urban ecology.
This Rebel Clash print by artist Ryota Murayama stages the flock-behavior flavor text directly in the art: two Pidove on a rooftop ledge above a warm urban landscape.

related_cards.name: Tranquill
related_cards.note: Stage-1 evolution (level 21), Unova regional bird line
